source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We're hiring an Account Manager to own client relationships and drive retention and expansion across a portfolio of B2B SaaS accounts. You'll be joining a function that's actively being built, and you'll co-shape what "great" looks like alongside your fellow AMs and our Chief Client Officer.

This role is accountable for client retention, net revenue retention, and overall client satisfaction. Success means turning SEO and content into measurable pipeline, securing renewals, and converting smart expansion opportunities into closed work.

This is a high-leverage role. We hire AMs who operate as the driving force on accounts — not participants in them. If you're great at coordinating but want someone else to own the narrative and the commercial outcome, this isn't the role for you. If you've been running accounts and are looking for the autonomy to set the standard, keep reading.

This role reports directly to the Chief Client Officer and has the opportunity to evolve into a team lead position as the function scales.

We require work hours to overlap at least 3 hours with US Eastern Time.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

Your responsibilities will include …

 - Own the relationship. Serve as the primary point of contact for senior stakeholders. If something is unclear on an account — who owns the deck, what the narrative should be, when the renewal motion starts — you make it clear. You don't wait for the strategist to bring it up.

 - Own the narrative. Bring the first draft of the deck, the QBR narrative, the renewal pitch, and the performance story. Strategists refine your draft, not the other way around. Translate SEO and content performance into executive-ready language that ties to pipeline, ARR, and the business outcomes your client cares about.

 - Own renewals end-to-end. You own the timeline, the prep, the deck, the narrative, the pricing conversation, and the close. Strategists support proof; you own the outcome. Forecast renewal value, build QBR/EBR decks, align on scope and budget, and manage change orders.

 - Drive expansion. Proactively identify qualified expansion opportunities across your healthy accounts. Frame it, pitch it, and follow through on execution. When you sell a new service or channel into an account, you own the execution path — getting the right people in the room, on the agenda, and tied to outcomes. "Sold" is the start of the work, not the end.

 - Lead executive conversations. Frame trade-offs, handle objections in the moment, reframe flat or ambiguous performance with honesty and a plan forward, and turn data into persuasive narratives that protect and grow accounts.

 - Run the engagement. Coordinate internal SEO, content, GEO, and outreach teams to ensure delivery predictability. QA deliverables, surface risks early, and provide high-level context before presenting to clients. Loop strategists in intentionally, not reflexively.

 - Manage escalations. Act as the first line for client issues. Diagnose, propose solutions with trade-offs, pull in Executive Sponsors when needed, and close the loop with a prevention plan.

 - Think across the portfolio. Patterns you spot at one account are usually relevant to others. Bring those threads to Pod

source_jobs.title: Paid Search Consultant
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT OMNISCIENT DIGITAL

Omniscient Digital is an AI-first B2B organic growth agency working with B2B brands from startups to large household enterprises like SAP, TikTok, and Adobe. We specialize in SEO, Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), digital PR, and content strategy.

We are building a paid media practice from the ground up. This is a foundational hire: you will be the first dedicated paid media strategist at Omniscient, responsible for proving the service line and establishing processes.


THE ROLE

You own paid search strategy, execution, and performance for an initial book 3 to 5 B2B clients. Google Ads is the main channel with Microsoft Ads/Bing in the mix.

You work alongside our organic strategists to build integrated growth programs where paid search and organic reinforce each other. You'll sit at the intersection of paid search, SEO, GEO, and content, using paid search to capture commercial intent at the moment it converts, and using everything we already know about our clients' organic and AI-search behavior to inform sharper paid strategy than agencies without that context can build. The application passcode is PAIDISAWESOME.


RESPONSIBILITIES

Operational Infrastructure

 - Set up Omniscient Digital's Google Ads MCC and Microsoft Ads agency account and define access policy

 - Define and implement client onboarding and offboarding procedures

 - Work with our Financial Controller to establish billing and invoicing flow

 - Implement QA, escalation, and risk procedures

Tech Stack

 - Google Ads direct vs. SA360

 - Tooling for budget management, reporting, and QA

 - Conversion tracking and attribution stack

Campaign Strategy and Execution

 - End-to-end paid search strategy on Google Ads (Search, Performance Max, YouTube, Display) and Microsoft Ads/Bing for B2B clients

 - Build, launch, and optimize full-funnel paid search programs tied to pipeline and revenue goals, not just leads and CPL

 - Manage client budgets ranging from $10K to $100K+ per month

 - Accountability for both efficiency metrics (ROAS, CPL, CAC) and pipeline-stage metrics (cost per opportunity, SQL conversion rate, pipeline velocity)

 - Structured testing programs across keywords, creatives, audiences, bidding strategies, and campaign structures

Integration with Organic Growth

 - Lead paid search strategy that uses our SEO, GEO, and content data as a competitive edge, keyword and SERP intelligence the organic team already builds becomes the sharpest input to your paid search account

 - Capture commercial-intent and bottom-funnel demand

 - Identify paid search opportunities informed by SEO and AI visibility research

 - Identify cross-sell opportunities within existing organic-only accounts and support business development with paid search audits and proposals

Reporting, Analysis, and Client Communication

 - Own weekly and monthly performance reporting with a clear narrative connecting paid search spend to pipeline impact

 - Present results and strategic recommendations to clients and internal stakeholders with business-level context, not just platform metrics

 - Build multi-touch attribution frameworks that tie paid search activity to pipeline and ARR, working with client marketing

source_jobs.title: Organic Growth Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We’re hiring an Organic Growth Analyst to support our client-facing strategists and help execute high-impact work across SEO and content..

This is an early-career role with room to grow. You’ll assist in research, reporting, and execution. You’ll learn how to build strategies that actually drive pipeline and revenue. And over time, you’ll have the chance to level up into a strategist role yourself.

You’ll report to an Organic Growth Strategist and work closely with the rest of our strategy and content teams. The application password is ORGANIC25.



This is a remote position, but you must have at least 5 hours of overlap with Eastern Time (ET) working hours.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

Your job as the Organic Growth Analyst would include …

 - Support the execution of SEO and organic growth strategies for B2B SaaS clients

 - Conduct keyword research, competitive analysis, content audits, and backlink research

 - Build and maintain reports and dashboards that show how strategies are performing

 - Track and analyze client data to identify insights and opportunities

 - Help ensure deliverables meet our internal standards and drive real results

 - Join team brainstorms and client calls to observe and learn

 - Take initiative on internal projects, research, and R&D efforts




TIME BREAKDOWN

Here’s how your time might shake out:

 - 50% on research, reporting, analysis, and task execution

 - 20% on team collaboration and internal updates

 - 20% on skill development and process improvements

 - 10% on supporting client communications and meetings




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 1 to 2 years of experience in SEO, content, or digital marketing, or a strong interest in building a career in this space

 - An analytical mindset with basic experience using tools like Google Analytics, Looker Studio, or Ahrefs

 - Excellent attention to detail and ability to follow through on tasks

 - Willingness to learn fast and take feedback

 - Clear, concise communicator who is comfortable asking questions

 - Comfortable working in a remote team with high standards and high trust

 - Availability that overlaps with at least 5 hours of ET time on weekdays




WHAT YOU’LL GET

We're here to help you do great work and grow personally and professionally.

 - A clear growth path toward a Strategist role

 - Hands-on experience in SEO, content strategy, analytics, and CRO

 - The chance to work with fast-growing SaaS brands and learn from experienced growth strategists

 - Autonomy to take on projects, explore ideas, and improve how we work

 - Confidence and skills that translate to in-house, agency, or freelance roles

 - Remote flexibility to work from anywhere

 - Coaching and upskilling opportunities

 - Fun team offsites to places all around the world (last year we went to Costa Rica)

 - Passcode: growthmarketing26




BENEFITS

We’re fortunate to offer competitive salaries and benefits, and we strive to create an environment that allows you to do your best work, develop your skills, and grow your career.

 - Health insurance - 90% of medical, dental, and vision insurance premiums paid for employees in the US

 - 401k with employer match - We’ll match 100% of your contributions up

source_jobs.title: Video Marketing Intern
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Video Marketing Intern to help us turn our ideas and expertise into high-impact video content.



We already have strong points of view, experienced creators, and no shortage of ideas. What we need is someone who can help bring those ideas to life through video. From shaping concepts and scripts to editing content that stands out on LinkedIn and YouTube.



You’ll work closely with our founders to produce educational, market-facing video content that helps Omniscient grow visibility across LinkedIn, YouTube, AI search, and classic SERPs.



This role is ideal for someone who loves video, understands social and YouTube-native formats, and wants hands-on experience building a modern B2B content engine.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Work with Founders to create dedicated educational videos on AEO, SEO, content, and digital PR (primarily talking head content, shot in person)

 - Turn long-form videos and podcasts into high-performing, shareable clips for LinkedIn and other platforms

 - Help translate high-level ideas into concrete video topics, scripts, and formats optimized for LinkedIn and YouTube

 - Edit video content to make it stand out (strong pacing, clean cuts, captions, light animations, and visual polish)

 - Proactively ideate new video concepts informed by customer conversations, market and competitor research, and SEO and AI search opportunities.

 - Help grow impressions and visibility across LinkedIn, YouTube, AI search, and organic search through consistent, high-quality video production

 - This is a part time internship of approximately 20 hours per week. Passcode: lovetoedit26
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - Comfortable editing video and excited about making content feel native to LinkedIn and YouTube

 - Strong instincts for what performs well on social and why

 - Able to take abstract ideas and turn them into clear, compelling video topics and scripts

 - Detail-oriented editor who can add captions, cuts, transitions, and light animations to make videos pop

 - Interested in B2B, marketing, content, SEO, or AI, you don’t need to be an expert, but you should be curious

 - Organized, reliable, and comfortable collaborating with senior teammates



 - Bonus (not required):
   
   - Experience with tools like Premiere Pro, Final Cut, After Effects, or similar
   
   - Familiarity with YouTube or LinkedIn analytics

Prior experience editing talking-head or podcast-style content

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can sometimes hold us back from applying for a job. But there’s no such thing as a "perfect" candidate. Omniscient Digital is a place where everyone can grow. However you identify and whatever background you bring, please apply if the idea of this role excites you.

Omniscient Digital is an equal opportunity employer. We ensure equal opportunity, and all candidates are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristics.

___

By submitting your application, you agree that Omniscient Digital may collect your personal data for recruiting, global org

source_jobs.title: Organic Growth Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.




ABOUT THIS ROLE

We’re looking for an Organic Growth Strategist who’s ready to drive measurable business results, experiment with novel approaches, and guide B2B brands toward revenue-generating SEO and organic growth strategies.

This isn’t just about rankings and traffic. You’ll focus on outcomes that matter — pipeline, conversions, and revenue — while helping us innovate how we deliver those results.

You’ll lead key client accounts, foster relationships with marketing execs, and work cross-functionally with our team to deliver foundational and cutting-edge growth initiatives.

While the position is remote, candidates must be based within the Eastern, Central, Mountain, or Pacific time zones.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Create impactful strategies. Own the roadmap for your portfolio of accounts. Build SEO strategies that focus on driving revenue and business outcomes.

 - Be the strategic quarterback. Lead client engagements by setting the vision, coordinating internal teams, and ensuring everything we deliver — from dashboards to CRO experiments — align with your strategy.

 - Foster stakeholder relationships. Build strong, long-term relationships with ambitious B2B brands. Help them see not just the “what” of our work but the “why” and how it impacts their bottom line.

 - Focus on analytics and attribution. Lean into reporting, data, and attribution to showcase the business impact of your work. Build dashboards, analyze results, and adjust strategies to get smarter and drive better outcomes.

 - Innovate and experiment. Stay on the cutting edge of what’s next in search and customer journeys. Contribute to internal R&D, explore smarter ways to deliver client value, and test new service offerings.

 - Ship high-quality work. Ensure client deliverables — from foundational SEO audits to creative new tools and campaigns — meet the highest standards and deliver measurable results.




TIME BREAKDOWN

Here’s how your time might shake out:

 - 40%: Deep dives into research, SEO audits, competitive analysis, and crafting strategies

 - 30%: Client-facing work — meetings, emails, and showing stakeholders why you’re the best thing that’s happened to their business

 - 15%: Leveling up your skills and experimenting with smarter ways to deliver excellent work

 - 15%: Team meetings and internal collaboration




WHO YOU ARE

 - You’re not just about traffic — you’re focused on driving revenue and business outcomes, and you’re excited to experiment with smarter, more impactful ways to achieve those goals.

 - You thrive on data. Attribution, reporting, and dashboards are your tools for showcasing impact and making strategic dec

source_jobs.title: Research Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We’re looking for a Research Analyst who can transform raw data into compelling narratives, strategic insights, and measurable business impact.

You love a spreadsheet, but you’re also the kind of person who looks at a dataset and sees a story that will get shared by CMOs and cited by journalists.

In this role, you’ll work on original research studies for Omniscient and on behalf of our clients that are cited, shared, and influence the industry narrative.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Design and lead original research (40%)

 - Develop research methodologies from scratch: survey design, sampling strategy, data collection, and statistical analysis

 - Analyze large datasets (often hundreds of thousands of data points) using Python, R, or similar tools to find patterns that matter

 - Identify and craft the data narrative. Work with stakeholders to turn data into a compelling story and narrative that achieves business outcomes.

 - Collaborate with editorial, digital PR, and SEO strategy to produce research reports that get cited, shared, and covered by industry media

Build visualizations and reporting (25%)

 - Create clear, compelling data visualizations that make complex findings accessible to non-technical audiences

 - Build and maintain BI dashboards (Looker, GA4) that give clients and internal teams actionable performance insights

 - Deliver research insights and data visualization in digestible, understanding, and shareable formats

Collaborate and consult (20%)

 - Partner with SEO strategists, content leads, and clients to identify research questions worth answering

 - Network with industry influencers, software and agency partners, and our broader ecosystem to partner on research studies, collect data, collaborate on campaigns, and drive distribution for the work.

 - Present findings to client stakeholders and translate data into strategic recommendations

Evolve the research practice (15%)

 - Experiment with new tools, methodologies, and data sources, especially at the intersection of AI, search, and content

 - Build repeatable research frameworks that scale across clients and topics

 - Expand our research practice into the industry gold standard




WHO YOU ARE


MUST-HAVES

 - You’ve designed and fielded original surveys or research studies (not just analyzed someone else’s data)

 - You can write and communicate well. Not just pull quotes from a dataset, you can write the narrative that frames findings for a business audience

 - Strong quantitative analysis skills: statistical methods, data cleaning, working with datasets of 1,000+ records

 - Proficiency in Python, R, or similar for data analysis (not just Excel, though that too)

 - Experience with data visualization. You know how to make a chart that communicates, not just decorates

 - Comfortable presenting findings to clients and non-technical stakeholders


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience with web analytics platforms (GA4, Looker, Search Console)

 - Familiarity with SEO, content marketing, or the B2B SaaS landscape

 - Published original research that earned media coverage or industry attention

 - Experience with NLP, LLM-related analysis, or AI search tools

 - Background in journalism, academic rese

source_jobs.title: Partnerships & Ecosystem Manager
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.

We’re seeking a Partnerships & Ecosystem Manager to expand and bring value to the Omniverse our gravitational orbit of agency partners, venture capital (VC) and private equity (PE) partners, software vendors, industry influencers, and co-marketing collaborators. While partnerships have historically driven some of our highest-quality leads, we haven’t yet invested the time or coordinated effort required to fully nurture and scale this motion. This role exists to change that.

As the Partnerships & Ecosystem Manager, you’ll drive structured partner engagement, co-marketing campaigns, events, and referral systems that both provide value to partners and generate meaningful network-sourced leads and visibility for Omniscient.

This is a role with ample support, including access to founders for partner introductions, podcast resources, original research campaigns, event budgets, and content amplifications.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

You will:

 - Build and manage an ecosystem of partners, across tier 1, 2, and 3 agency partners, software vendors, VC/PE firms, influencers, and other strategic collaborators.

 - Drive real partnership value by facilitating lead referrals, co-branded content collaborations, educational workshops, and thought leadership showcases.

 - Host and attend in-person events including dinners, meet-ups, panels, industry conferences, and networking events.

 - Coordinate with internal marketing to align partnership activities with content, podcast, research, and webinar efforts, amplifying reach and impact.

 - Track, measure, and optimize partner performance by defining KPIs, maintaining partner pipelines, and driving continuous improvement.

 - Support founders and leadership by managing follow-ups, partner communication, and execution logistics.
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

You might be a great fit if you:

 - Have experience or strong interest in partnership development, ecosystems, business development, or community engagement.

 - Are comfortable managing relationships, both external (partners, events, influencers) and internal (marketing, content, founders).

 - Are organized, proactive, and capable of juggling multiple projects and partners simultaneously.

 - Thrive in a fast-paced startup/agency environment where ownership and initiative are rewarded.

 - Are based on the East Coast (ideally NYC) or able to travel frequently for events.

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can sometimes hold us back from applying for a job. But there’s no such thing as a "perfect" candidate. Omniscient Digital is a place where everyone can grow. However you identify and whatever background you bring, please apply if the idea of this role excites you.

Omniscient Digital is an equal opportunity employer. We ensure equal opportunity, and all candidates are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristics.

___

By submitting your application, you agree that Omniscient Digital may collect your personal data for recruiting, global organization planning, and related purposes. Omniscient Digital’

source_jobs.title: Graphic Designer
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We're looking for a freelance graphic designer to help our work look as good as it reads. You'll take complex, often unglamorous B2B material (data, frameworks, research findings) and turn it into design people actually want to look at, across both digital and print.

Most of what we produce is for clients, so you'll be working inside their brand guidelines as often as ours. One week you might be laying out a print magazine with our editorial team; the next you're turning a messy sketch into a clean diagram, or designing a research report that a CMO will actually share.

This is a contract, fully remote role. We run on autonomy, so you'll own your projects end to end and we'll stay out of your way, as long as the work is great and you keep us in the loop.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Design report and ebook PDFs (whitepapers, research reports, ebooks) that hold up to both the client's brand standards and ours.

 - Turn data into visuals that make the insight obvious, not just the chart pretty.

 - Build custom diagrams from rough sketches, taking a half-formed concept and making it land in a single look.

 - Lead print magazine design, partnering with our editorial team on layouts worth keeping.

 - Create social and digital assets that stay consistent across a client's channels.

 - Run several projects at once and ship them on time without us having to chase you.

 - Work closely with marketers, writers, and editors so the design and the words pull in the same direction.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have a portfolio that shows range across digital (social, web assets) and even print (magazines, reports).

 - You know Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign, Photoshop) and Figma.

 - You sweat the details and can take a rough idea all the way to a polished, professional piece.

 - You take feedback well and turn it into better work, not just edits.

 - You juggle multiple deadlines without the quality slipping.

 - You actually like working with cross-functional teams, and you get that design serves the strategy, not the other way around.

 - You're self-directed. You set your own schedule, own your projects, and flag where things stand before anyone has to ask.

 - You learn as you go, ask for help when you need it, and offer it when someone else does. You make the road by walking.

 - You're not an asshole. We spend a lot of our days engaging with team members and clients. There's no room for assholes. You're friendly, eager to jump in and take ownership, and love to work as a team to be successful.




WHY YOU'LL LIKE WORKING WITH US

 - Real autonomy. You manage your own time and your own projects. We hire people we don't have to manage.

 - Interesting problems. B2B doesn't have to be boring. You'll get to make dry, technical material genuinely good-looking.

 - Sharp collaborators. Our editorial and strategy teams have done this at companies like HubSpot, Shopify, and Workato, and they care about the work.

 - Remote, always. Work from wherever you want.




HOW OUR HIRING PROCESS WORKS

We review applications on a rolling basis and aim to get back to you within a few weeks, though it can sometimes take a little longer. If it looks like a fit, we'll reach out by e

source_jobs.title: Editorial Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We're looking for an Editorial Strategist who can internalize a client's brand, product, and competitive position and use that depth to produce and direct high-impact work across written and creative formats.

This isn't a traditional editor or content manager role. The Editorial Strategist sits close to clients, diagnoses problems, and comes up with creative solutions: written content, original research, designed assets, UX copy, ad copy, and more. The title says "editorial" because the foundation is still narrative: understanding how to tell a story, structure an argument, and communicate complex ideas clearly. And of course, you still need solid editorial chops, but the scope goes well beyond words on a page.

You'll work within our Creative function, which fuels every revenue-driving channel at the agency — on-page/organic growth, digital PR, and paid media. Your job is to understand what the channel strategists need, bring creative depth and original thinking to the table, and produce (or direct the production of) the assets that make those strategies succeed.

Candidates must be based -/+ 3 hours of Eastern Time.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Think strategically about client businesses. Own the creative strategy for your accounts. You'll be close enough to your clients' products and positioning to diagnose problems and propose solutions — not just execute briefs and content. When a client's landing page isn't converting, you don't just rewrite the copy to rank; you architect a solution that might involve copy, design, UX, or all three.

 - Produce and direct creative work across formats. Blog content and landing pages are table stakes. You'll also work on original research reports, data visualizations, ad copy, brand narratives, UX/UI copy, interactive tools, and other formats we haven't even invented yet. You don't need to be an expert in all of these today — but you need to be the kind of person who figures it out, often with AI as your co-pilot (not as your replacement).

 - Fuel the channel strategists. Work closely with channel strategists to understand what they need and produce the creative assets that make their strategies work — whether that's a content roadmap for organic, a research report that earns links for digital PR, or landing page copy for paid campaigns.

 - Use AI as an accelerant. We don't layer AI onto old processes — we rethink processes from scratch with AI as a native tool. You'll use AI to handle the compositional and operational work (drafting, editing, formatting, research synthesis) so you can spend your time on the strategic and creative work that AI can't do: original insight, brand judgment, narrative architecture, and opportunity diagnosis.

 - Own your clients' creative outcomes. You'll own the creative quality and strategic relevance of everything that goes out the door for your accounts. Manage project-level budgets, make contractor decisions, and operate with the autonomy of someone who understands both the creative work and the business context behind it.

 - Ship high-quality work. Ensure every creative asset — from a blog post to a research report to an ad variant — meets the highest standards and delivers measurable results. Qual

source_jobs.title: Team Lead, Organic Growth Strategy
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We're hiring a Team Lead in Organic Growth Strategy: someone who can run a small group of Strategists while still owning their own book of clients. The split is 50/50 by design. Leadership here doesn't graduate you out of the work - it's done alongside it, by people who still ship great work themselves.

Our Strategy function has scaled to the size where leadership needs to sit close to the work. Team Leads are how we keep quality and team health intact as we grow. You'll be one of two reporting into the Director of Organic Growth Strategy, with a small group of Strategists looking to you for coaching, QA, and direction.

This is a hands-on role. You'll still be doing strategy, QA, and client work - just at half the volume so you can develop the people doing the same. If you want a route out of the craft and into pure management, this is the wrong job.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop a group of Strategists. You'll run bi-weekly 1:1s with each person, give honest feedback on their work and their growth, and coach the skills that separate good strategists from great ones: analytical depth, business judgment, research instincts, and the ability to turn insight into client outcomes. When deadlines slip or quality drops, you have the conversation.
   
   

 - Hold the quality bar for your team. You're accountable for what your Strategists ship. That doesn't mean reviewing every output. It means knowing which work is risky enough to need your eyes, which Strategists need closer support right now, and which deliverable types you haven't seen done well yet. Selective QA done well beats blanket review every time.
   
   

 - Run your own clients at full strength. Half your week is your own book of accounts. The bar is the same one any senior individual contributor in the function carries: deep client knowledge, sharp strategy, and delivery you can be proud of. Leadership doesn't soften the expectations on your own work.
   
   

 - Help build the team. You'll screen and interview candidates for Strategist roles, give clear hiring input, and own the onboarding of new Strategists into your group. The team you inherit is the team you've helped pick.
   
   

 - Make the function repeatable. You'll spot the workflow friction that keeps showing up, document what good looks like, and contribute to SOPs that let us scale without diluting the work. When the Director kicks off process work, you're a meaningful part of executing it.
   
   

 - Use AI like it matters. AI is a core part of how we produce strategy, not a bolt-on. You'll bring that mindset to your team: building workflows that move faster without losing rigor, and coaching your Strategists on where AI accelerates their thinking versus where their judgment still has to drive the work. Passcode: growthleader26
   
   


TIME BREAKDOWN

Roughly how the week shakes out:

 - 50%: Client delivery on your own book

 - 20%: 1:1s, coaching, feedback, and performance conversations

 - 15%: Selective QA and quality oversight across your team's accounts

 - 10%: Hiring, onboarding, and process work

 - 5%: Cross-functional collaboration, internal meetings, and your own development




WHO YOU ARE

 - You're a senior Strategist ready to

source_jobs.title: Digital PR Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We're looking for a Digital PR Specialist who understands that off-page strategy in 2026 is about much more than link building.

The channels that influence how brands get discovered are shifting: LLMs are citing sources, Reddit threads are ranking on page one, and brand mentions carry weight even without a hyperlink. We need someone who can operate across all of it.

This role sits within our Off-Page/Digital PR function, one of three client-facing channel teams at the agency (alongside On-Page/Organic Growth and emerging Paid Media).

You'll work closely with our Outreach team and Director of Digital PR to plan and execute earned media campaigns that build client authority across search engines, AI platforms, and the communities where their buyers spend time.

The foundation of some projects may still be link acquisition, but the aperture is wider: brand mention outreach, original research distribution, Reddit engagement strategy, and digital PR campaigns that earn coverage because the story is worth telling.

You're not blasting templated emails to webmasters. You're building relationships with journalists, editors, community moderators, and industry voices. You're pitching stories grounded in original data. You're identifying the Reddit threads and niche communities where a client's expertise adds real value. You understand that every earned placement, whether it carries a backlink or not, shapes how a brand shows up in traditional and AI-powered search.

While the position is remote, candidates must be based +/- 3 hours of Eastern Time.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and manage link acquisition campaigns. Plan, execute, and iterate on link-building campaigns across your portfolio of accounts. This includes prospecting high-authority, editorially relevant sites; crafting personalized pitches that lead with value; securing placements that move the needle on domain authority and referral traffic. You know the difference between a link that matters and one that doesn't.

 - Run brand mention and digital PR outreach. Identify and pursue unlinked brand mentions, product citations, and earned media opportunities. Pitch client stories, data, and expertise to journalists, bloggers, newsletter authors, and podcast hosts. As LLMs increasingly use brand mentions and citations to determine authority, this work directly influences how clients show up in AI-generated answers.

 - Distribute original research and data-driven content. Work with the Creative team to package and promote original research reports, surveys, and data studies. Build outreach campaigns that turn proprietary data into earned coverage, backlinks, and brand authority. Original research is one of the most effective earned media assets we produce; your job is to make sure the right people see it.

 - Develop and execute Reddit engagement strategies. Map relevant subreddits for each client, understand community norms and moderation expectations, and develop engagement playbooks that position clients as genuine contributors. This isn't about dropping links; it's about building credibility in the communities where buyers are already asking questions.

 - Collaborate across channel teams. Work with On-Page/Organic

source_jobs.title: Senior Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Omniscient is an organic growth agency that builds revenue-focused organic growth systems for B2B software companies — optimized for both search engines and generative AI.

We pride ourselves on being lean, agile, and experimental. Our team thrives on R&D and innovation, always exploring the smartest ways to deliver exceptional results. We believe in a culture of building and shipping — whether it’s client deliverables, new services, or internal tools that keep us ahead of the game.

If you’re looking for a role where you’ll tackle challenging problems, work with ambitious brands, and help shape the future of organic growth, let’s talk.

Learn about the principles https://beomniscient.com/principles/ that drive how we work and build a company.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

You'll own how Omniscient hires. As we scale the team and launch new service lines, the people we bring in shape the company we become, and you'll be the person responsible for finding them, qualifying them, and closing them. This is a full-cycle role: you own each search from first outreach to signed offer.

This is not a coordination job. You won't wait for applicants to roll in or for someone else to define the search. You'll source proactively, build qualified pipelines from scratch, run screens, partner with hiring managers as an equal, and close the candidates we want. You'll own our Ashby instance and the entire recruiting process, and you'll improve both as you go. You're the only recruiter, so you own every req across the company and the judgment that comes with it.

You'll report to our CEO and work directly with hiring managers across Strategy, Editorial, Digital PR, and Operations.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own full-cycle recruiting for every open role (technical and non-technical), from defining the search to a signed offer, across all departments at once.

 - Source proactively. Build a network of passive candidates and develop a sourcing strategy for each role rather than waiting for inbound.

 - Write fantastic job postings, and recommend changes when the wrong candidates are coming through.

 - Screen, interview, and keep candidates moving. Run phone pre-screens, coordinate interviews, and follow up with everyone.

 - Close candidates. Sell the role, manage comp, and navigate counteroffers and cold feet.

 - Partner with hiring managers to prioritize needs, push back on unrealistic criteria, and build a seamless interview process.

 - Own our Ashby instance and recruiting data. Keep the funnel accurate and build out new and existing processes as you go.

 - Track weekly and monthly recruiting metrics, and maintain dashboards that give real-time visibility into pipeline health and hiring performance.

 - Surface insights from the data and flag at-risk searches early, serving as the go-to resource for recruiting reporting.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have senior, full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally at a fast-paced marketing or creative agency with many reqs.

 - You're a proactive sourcer first. You can describe exactly how you find people who aren't applying, and you have the hires to prove it.

 - You've closed candidates yourself: comp conversations, counteroffers, and talking someone back in when they got cold feet.

 - You've run multiple searches at once and can explain the system that kept them all moving.

 - You've actually administered Ashby (or a similar ATS like Greenhouse or Lever), not just clicked through it.

 - You're meticulous and detail-obsessed. Dropped candidates and messy pipelines are unacceptable to you. (the passcode is CLOSER2026)

 - You partner with hiring managers rather than taking orders. You'll tell them when their criteria don't add up, and be right often enough that they trust you.

 - You're autonomous. You don't wait to be told what to do, and you communicate proactively about where things stand.

 - You write and speak English fluently, enough to run closing conversations with senior candidates.

 - You're not
